---
title: Contract wizard
description: Customise a token, NFT, or counter with feature toggles and a live preview, then scaffold a compile-ready project.
---

The contract wizard builds a contract from your choices: pick a type, set its
parameters, toggle features on and off, and watch the source update live. When it
looks right, scaffold it straight into a new project.

Open it from **Projects → Customise a contract**.

## Build a contract

<Steps>

<Step title="Pick a contract type">
  Choose Fungible Token, NFT, Stablecoin, or Counter from the selector. The
  parameters and feature toggles change to match the type.
</Step>

<Step title="Set parameters">
  Fill in the fields, name, symbol, premint, license, access control. Each type
  shows only the fields that apply to it.
</Step>

<Step title="Toggle features">
  Switch features on and off. The preview updates and the file tabs change as
  features add or remove functions. Hover the info icon on a toggle to see exactly
  which functions it adds.
</Step>

<Step title="Add custom functions (optional)">
  Append typed function stubs with **Add custom function**. Each becomes a
  compiling `pub fn` with a `// TODO` body.
</Step>

<Step title="Create the project">
  Name it and click **Create project**. The wizard scaffolds the full source tree
  and opens it in the editor, ready to compile and deploy.
</Step>

</Steps>

## Contract types

<CardGrid>

<Card title="Fungible Token" icon="layers">
  An SEP-41 token from OpenZeppelin's Stellar contracts. Set name, symbol, and
  premint; toggle Mintable, Burnable, Pausable, and Upgradeable.
</Card>

<Card title="NFT" icon="layers">
  A non-fungible collection. Toggle Mintable, Burnable, Enumerable, Pausable, and
  Upgradeable.
</Card>

<Card title="Stablecoin" icon="layers">
  A fungible token with optional allowlist or blocklist transfer controls.
</Card>

<Card title="Counter" icon="hammer">
  WebSoroban's own stateful counter, assembled from feature fragments. Toggle
  Decrementable, Ownable, Resettable, and Pausable.
</Card>

</CardGrid>

The first three are generated by `@openzeppelin/wizard-stellar` and emit an
OpenZeppelin **workspace** (the deploy target is the member crate, e.g.
`fungible-contract`). The Counter is a single-crate project. Both produce the same
bundle the scaffolder uses everywhere else.

## Feature dependencies

The wizard never lets you generate an invalid combination. Two rules apply:

- **Access control is required by some features.** On a token or NFT, Mintable,
  Pausable, and Upgradeable need an owner. If Access control is set to None, the
  wizard promotes it to Ownable and tells you.
- **Some features require others.** On the Counter, Pausable and Resettable
  require Ownable, so enabling either turns Ownable on and locks it.

<Callout type="note" title="Locked toggles">
  A toggle that another feature depends on is switched on and disabled, with a
  "Required by …" note. Turn off the dependent feature to free it.
</Callout>

## Custom functions

A custom function is a typed stub, not arbitrary logic. Give it a snake_case name,
add arguments (each with a type like `u32`, `Address`, or `String`), and pick a
return type. The wizard appends a compiling function to the contract:

```rust title="appended to the contract"
pub fn airdrop(env: soroban_sdk::Env, to: soroban_sdk::Address, amount: i128) {
    // TODO: implement this function
}
```

The `env` argument is added automatically. Names that collide with the contract's
generated functions, Rust keywords, or duplicate arguments are rejected inline.

## Save, reuse, and export

<CardGrid>

<Card title="Save as my template" icon="layers">
  Save the design under **My Templates**. Re-open it to keep customising, or click
  **Use** to scaffold a project from it directly.
</Card>

<Card title="Export" icon="upload">
  Download the single contract file, or the whole project as a `.zip`.
</Card>

</CardGrid>

## Deploy

Wizard contracts deploy out of the box. The generated tokens, NFTs, and the
Ownable Counter take a constructor, when you deploy, WebSoroban supplies your own
wallet address for every address parameter (owner, recipient), so the contract
instantiates without extra steps.

<Callout type="warning" title="Testnet only">
  Everything the wizard produces targets Stellar testnet. There is no mainnet path.
</Callout>

## Next

- Build and read diagnostics in [Compile](/docs/compile).
- Ship it in [Deploy](/docs/deploy), then call it from [Invoke](/docs/invoke).
- Browse the ready-made [templates](/docs/templates).
